items.source_finding_or_category: FP-07 (Fase 4 risk register) — .backup files are redundant snapshots of legacy/wake/*.py
items.rationale: .backup extension indicates it was created as a safety snapshot before refactoring. The original tts.py is not present (only the backup). If the original is gone, backup serves no purpose. Git history preserves the content. Two commits, both in the legacy isolation era.
items.false_positive_risk: MEDIUM — verify whether tts.py still exists before removing backup; backup may be the only copy
items.dependencies: Check: does legacy/wake/tts.py exist without .backup suffix?

items.source_finding_or_category: FP-02 (Fase 4 risk register) — name 'tart=always' looks like a CLI argument captured as filename
items.rationale: Untracked file. Name with '=' character is highly anomalous — matches pattern 'tart=always' as if from a command like '--restart=always' tail. 1700B text. Not in git. Low false-positive risk.
items.false_positive_risk: LOW — no plausible legitimate source file would be named tart=always
items.rollback: Not applicable — untracked file; if needed, restore from local backup

items.last_commit: git log returns EMPTY — file is in git ls-files but has no commit history visible
items.source_finding_or_category: Fase 4A exclusion list v2 item 1 — tracked accidental shell output artifact; shell metacharacter name
items.rationale: Tracked in git (git ls-files confirms presence) but git log returns empty, suggesting it was added in a commit but the history is compacted or the filename caused log parsing issues. Filename is a POSIX character class fragment from a shell command (tr/sed). ASCII text with overstriking is characteristic of pager/man output captured accidentally. 15915 bytes — same size as 'how --stat --summary 5dda82a', suggesting they are related pager capture artifacts. MEDIUM risk because git rm requires careful quoting.
items.false_positive_risk: VERY LOW — no legitimate file would be named '[:alnum:] \n \'
items.special_handling: Filename requires careful shell quoting. Use -- separator and single quotes.

Filename is the literal tail of a git command: 'git show --stat --summary 5dda82a' → the 'how --stat --summary 5dda82a' fragment. 15915 bytes ASCII with overstriking = pager output captured accidentally to a file. Same size as F5-011 — likely paired artifacts from the same incident.
items.false_positive_risk: VERY LOW — no legitimate file would be named 'how --stat --summary 5dda82a'
items.special_handling: Filename requires careful shell quoting. Use -- separator and single quotes.

items.source_finding_or_category: F4-FIND-003 / FP-01 — large PostScript binary in experiments/, likely accidental man/pager capture
items.rationale: 20.9MB PostScript file named 'genai'. Untracked. Could be an accidental capture of a PostScript manual (e.g., `man genai | ps2pdf`) or intentional reference material. BLOCKED pending operator verification of content origin and intent.
items.false_positive_risk: MEDIUM — could be legitimate reference material if operator placed it intentionally
items.dependencies: Unknown — could be referenced by experiments
items.block_reason: F4-FIND-003 requires operator decision before any action. Operator must confirm content is not intentional reference material.

items.source_finding_or_category: F4-FIND-002 — nested Git repository not declared as submodule
items.rationale: Supply chain decision required. 42 files in directory, nested .git at source/.git. Not a declared submodule (.gitmodules is absent). Entire directory is untracked in Project_ARIS. Options: (a) declare as git submodule, (b) vendor with manifest and remove .git dir, (c) remove after operator approval, (d) quarantine in excluded/ dir.
items.false_positive_risk: HIGH — this is an intentional MCP candidate that operator placed here
items.dependencies: Potential MCP integration dependency — verify before any action
proposed_future_options: vendor_with_manifest: remove source/.git, add vendor manifest JSON, track files directly
quarantine: mv to excludent/ or annotate in EXCLUDENT_POLICY.md
items.block_reason: F4-FIND-002 supply chain decision — operator must choose integration strategy before any action.

items.source_finding_or_category: F4-FIND-001 — resolved as false positive; .env was never git-tracked
items.rationale: F4-FIND-001 was resolved as false positive: .env was never committed. git_tracked=True in the Fase 1 dataset was a scanner bug (git ls-files --others without --exclude-standard). .gitignore has .env on line 1 and *.env on line 6. File contains local configuration — operator must decide on secret rotation independently.
items.dependencies: Local runtime configuration — must not be deleted
items.block_reason: DO NOT TOUCH — F4-FIND-001 closed as false positive. .env is correctly gitignored. Rotation is operator responsibility.
